extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR Tadej attacked 38km out on penultimate Cat-1 climb, soloed to victory while Quintana lost podium by not responding to Valverde's late pace increase.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR attacked 38km from finish during early hesitation on penultimate climb, not waiting for steeper final ramps as expected.
VALVERDE rode for podium time gaps in final 6km without attacking, forcing Quintana to chase while Roglic wheel-sucked.
Movistar called QUINTANA to front with 6km left to defend third, but he couldn't match Valverde's sustained pace.
UAE kept POGACAR tucked/drafting entire descent despite solo lead, prioritizing aero gains over power display.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: At 20 years old, attacked 38km out when expected to wait, handled windy descent solo, already signed 5-year UAE extension
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost podium position to teammate Valverde by failing to respond when called to front with 6km remaining
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Rode calculated tempo in final 6km specifically to gain time on Pogacar without attacking, reclaimed podium from Quintana
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Sat on Valverde's wheel refusing to work despite teammate Lopez behind, purely defensive riding to protect 2:33 lead
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: Lost podium contention after teammates Quintana/Valverde rode for themselves, grimacing and unable to follow final pace
